title: Mildly Aerated Ukrainian Corn Pilaf
description: A traditional Ukrainian dish featuring dried white corn and a miso marinade, this pilaf is both fatty and aerated, making it a unique and flavorful experience. It is also Buddhist-temple-friendly, making it accessible to a wide range of diets.
instructions: Rehydrate the dried white corn by soaking it in water overnight, then drain and set aside.
In a blender or food processor, combine the miso paste, vegetable oil, onion, carrot, garlic, salt, black pepper, and dried parsley, and blend until smooth.
In a large skillet, heat the blended miso mixture over medium heat, stirring constantly, until it starts to bubble and emit a fragrant aroma.
Add the rehydrated corn to the skillet and stir to combine with the miso mixture.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the corn is tender and the mixture is lightly aerated.
Stir in the lemon juice and continue to simmer for an additional 5 minutes.
Remove the skillet from the heat and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the pilaf hot, garnished with additional dried parsley if desired.
